column33: Comments

Sadly the only Invisible Woman card I saw was the rare (which I did recruit). Black Widow and Emma Frost were great heroes for this setup. (They are typically great in these tournaments,  but this setup had them thrive.)
It was a ton of fun. Being able to focus on a single hero class made quick work of Galactus but if you did not do that it would have been a very different game.

First off, I didn't realize that I needed to record what was left in the violation deck. I did the best that I could to recreate it, but it's probably a little off. 

First game went really well. Got 2 copies of Unstable Molecules and 2 copies of One Gigantic Hand and was hitting Galactus once (or twice) pretty much every turn after that. Only had one villain escape, a monster burrowed right before Galactus ate the Streets. I don't think there's anyone who pairs with Mr Fantastic besides Iron Man (which kinda fits)

column33: Had a bagel shuffle start master strike.  Mr F and Iron man rocked hand one, with Iron Man's ult early on.  Was drawing his whole deck on most hands with a tech core and took out the big G fast.  Totally fun setup.  Didn't bother with a second attempt.

I won both games but was able to win 1 turn quicker on the second game with the hands closer together in VP so I went with that one.  First game I had 4 master strikes by turn 16, second game only 1 master strike so as a result, only 1 villain escaped. Plus while I had 6 scheme twists come out, only the last 2 really KO'd any heroes so I wasn't that tense about losing to scheme twists. 

This was fun though, I first saw Galactus and prior to this had never beaten him so I thought I was going to lose but when I found out card draw is the way to go, I was able to leverage that into wins.
Galactus got melked
Fun game.  2 good hits on Galactus which really upset him and he followed up with a nice run of Master Strikes flooding the city.

It made it exciting! Especially when by turn 7 three master strikes had already happened....and we still almost prevailed!
help

Very fun set up, a bit easy with Jean Grey + Bishop and Shatterstar + Warpath + Logan, Strange was helpful too. I had almost every rare but some extra recruit slowed me down at the end! Love Dark City with Messiah Complex!

First attempt was slightly quicker with same VP total but had a lot of escapes. I went with 2nd attempt because it had zero escapes. I was KOing like crazy, so much that I had to be careful not to KO all my recruit too soon! Regarding Villain deck tally, Mr Sinister took a Bystander and I had 1 Fight the Future.
Didn’t expect to pair warpath and Jean so well. But it was a pleasant surprise.

Superior Iron Man and Daredevil absolutely rocked socks in this setup. Paired with Warpath's investigation and screening, there was so much synergy for what they did and we ended up really successful!

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Lots of fun with this one. P2 was able to create a big card draw engine between Bishop and X-Force Wolvie, whom seemed perfect for this pod. That took care of Mr. Sinister pretty quickly.
Both games had a stream of Dr. strange heroes in the villain deck before I got any momentum going. In my 2nd game I had 8 heroes KO’d by the 14th turn. This moved much faster than I expected.

My first attempt was pretty amazing, especially turn 27. I managed to buy the rare of Jean Grey from HQ, draw my entire deck by rescueing bystanders (from Jean Grey's 3-cost) and drawing 2 and later even 3 cards with Jean Grey's 6-cost (her last one was also in the deck/discard pile). My entire deck was full of X-men, so I ended up being able to rescue 25 bystanders, but the stack only held 21 at that moment (the pile was already slightly smaller from earlier rescues and ambush effects). Sinister's sinister plans couldn't defeat the Jean Grey, Bishop, Iceman powerhouse. I didn't even count the attack from that last turn precisely, as it was clear I had much more than I needed (and the exact counting was tedious - 3 of iceman's 4-costs with around 18 [Ranged] cards before the first). One of the earlier turns had a similar wombo combo, but slightly more focused at recruit with Iceman's 2-costs. Which was very useful. I feel like I made a good choice with Jean Grey because there were many bystanders from the setup (Master Striker/Mastermind Tactics/Villains/Hecnhmen). Iceman also really shone. If you want to know. my second attempt wasn't as good as the first (which would be hard, because it probably had to also empty or almost empty the Bystander stack), but needed only 1 more turn. So I submitted my first.

I like getting high scores for my BG Stats app, but it's usually pretty dependent on setups that create huge VP Masterminds & Villain Decks, which wasn't the case here as much. 77 is a pretty great score for 1 hand/deck, but it's not my best ever. It was my first time (or at least since I can remember) that I managed to empty the Bystander Stack, so that's a cool first (although normally the stack is much bigger, so it wouldn't be as easy). Scheme felt pretty shuffle-based, but the shuffle was fine I think, with 2 escpaes early on (Clone from henchmen pushed 2 earlier clones out of the city), which also K.O. 2 non grey heroes, I managed to damage control to 4 K.O.'s from twists.

Deckbuilding for both decks was pretty good from the get go. I made use of Clone for both to get certain cheap cards mulitple times quickly. K.O.-ing went great, Hand 1 lost 11 starter cards before turn 27 and then its last in turn 27 (which didn't matter), Hand 2 did almost as good, but didn't build quite as many cards, but was pretty consistent and strong enough to fight things. Deck 1 was mostly Ranged/Covert with lots of Jean Grey, Iceman and Bishop. Deck 2 had Bishops rare pretty early on and managed to clone Old Man Logan's 3-cost twice and Warpath's Instinct 2-cost once. Deck 2 was pretty much an instinct fest with Warpath and Old Man Logan. Both decks could build pretty well without needing the same cards and Clone was a great aid there.

It was a pretty fun setup, never feeling too tense because losing could happen any turn, or like being way overpowered. As usal, building for very synergistic decks makes for some super strong turns which then close out the game really quick. If you'd rewind a few turns, you wouldn't guess it was almost won, but once you catch some drift those strong turns can even be back to back.

Think I did ok on this one, but those 2 Villain escapes may hurt my score. Warpath can get pretty fun when you have 10 card hands to start each turn (which also bypasses Mr. Sinister's Master Strikes) =)
Fun!
I can always count on a red head to help me out.

I forgot to count my remaining cards in the first attempt and know that I did better. Ahhh, so frustrating! But, did enjoy this setup with awesome combos with Bishop, Magik and Iceman. The set up for the villains quickly escalated with the Mr. Sinister clones and all of my KO heroes were a result of the escaped villains. Ended up with 8 KO'd heroes (9 to lose) on both attempts. Hopefully, I qualify for round 2!

Giant Ego (Ant-man) worked very well with Jocasta ant Ant-Man, and Hail of Bullets defeated for free a lot of Villains. First hand mostly just cleaned up the HQ and did some damage with Star-Lord and its shards.

A lot of interplay with each of the different classes. Our first Bageltop Legendary tournament. We enjoyed the process of being able to choose two heroes in a way that we can maximize this particular setup. We look forward to the upcoming set-ups and weeks!

column33: Doing danger sense and shards won the game for me being able to manipulate things downwards saved me

I used the same strategy on both attempts. 1st hand went hard on a tech draw strategy with Iron man, Nova, and Totally Awesome Hulk. MVP card was Nova's "Declare Galactic Threat", since with a ton of tech cards, it allowed me to keep the city clear. It was also easy to acquire when it was put in the city by a scheme twist. The 2nd hand used a covert/ranged build with Black Cat, Nova, and Iron Man. It was not as effective, but had tons of recruit, which made it easy to refresh the HQ.

2nd attempt also won, but took longer to get going, so had more escapes and took longer. Overall a fun setup.
I was able to use the extra turn I got from Kosmos to clear the City, then gain the Tactic that takes Shards from Villains in the City. Awesome interaction, you should try it

I figured Captain Marvel and Nova would pair well with AKP and Cho-Hulk and they did pretty well. Went with Tech/Covert on one side and Ranged/Strength on the other. I wasn't able to thin my decks very much so they felt very bloated but nonetheless was able to pull off a win. Magus and the Scheme Twists kept the city full near constantly. I wish I had brought someone to help clear shards from villains more effectively.
It was a great set up for the first round!

This felt tough. We won both games, but picking heroes never had me saying "this is clearly the answer" like I have in previous setups. It made choosing heroes difficult, but also Adam Warlock's Rare couldn't hurt us so we went with that.

Game changing moment when The Shaper of Worlds created a pocket dimension for Captain Mar-vell's rare saving it from the Scheme Twist. Once I got that into my deck, I used its danger sense to control the villain deck. I honestly wasn't spending shards enough. Final blow got blocked for 2 turns by Magus requiring stars to fight for the last tactic.
The city was relentless, so to avoid escapes I prioritized accumulating attack. Kitty Pryde's Phase Out was the MVP of both games.
1st time playing, need to track all results better next round. Enjoyed the set-up.
I was 1 fight short of winning. I am very frustrated by this loss.
Fun Setup.

Game one went great other than one escape at the beginning caused by the trap. We were very fortunate to avoid most of the scheme twists. Ended up building one deck based on strength wounds and the other on ranged wounds which worked out well.

Game 1 = Absurd. I think every feast hit a non-grey hero. Heckin' Rood. 
Game 2 = Not nearly enough Iceman, but at least the Villain Deck played more in my favor.

The first attempt using Jean Grey and Human Torch and it was a disaster, couldn't even generate any high attack hands.  Switched Jean Grey out with Blade and we had much better results.  Good attacks when needed.  the MM and villains feasting helped weed out my starters, so that was nice.  I enjoyed the setup.
Won on the last turn.  It was an interesting and fun challenge.

column33:
"It was what it was".
We chose our heroes by the chance to convert our wounds into active gameplay (smash, KO wounds, etc.). The high amount of Feast (given enemies + Miek) led to high rng in KOing our own decks. In the between we were focussing to much on buying the right cards, to transform our heroes early, which staled our damage output due to bad hero draw rng.
The mastermind was cool-ish. The four horseman sucked (destroy your deck, destroy your deck even more). The scheme was easy to overcome, just costed us a few rounds, where we only bought shards from the mastermind instead of new heroes (which were decimated from too many Feasts). The first 4 twists came within the first 10 rounds, after that we had to manage a lot of enemies, while we didn't fight many of the four hoursemen (due to their bad effects).
It was our first time with transforming mastermind and transforming heroes.
We are unsatisfied with our result, but playing another round is even worse.

This was tough, but decently fun. First game started rough, had a bunch of escapes early, and couldn’t quite recover from all those wounds. Had much better luck the second game, and was able to assemble a great ranged deck with Iceman and Namora (even pulled off Bishop’s double super power on the last turn). Right hand wasn’t anything special, as made apparent by VP differential.

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33:
You sneaky boys, I didn't look at what I was getting into until I was about to shuffle it all together and I saw just how many heroes were going to be KOd through FEAST and Four Horseman effects. I went with Skaar because I figured his wounded fury cards would benefit from all the wounds we would be getting. Turns out after 36 turns of the game, I only ever got to use Wounded Fury on the very last turn to end the game. Every other time, I'd never draw the necessary cards to trigger the ability.

I went with Silver Surfer for the other hero on a whim. I didn't put much thought into it other than "Focus is nice", but in the end, it dominated. And it also circumvented the Four Hoursmen fight effects with that one card that lets you defeat villains of 5 or 6 power for free. 

In the end, I got lucky on a lot of early recruits and some luck with making over 20 recruit through the Silver Surfer rare and buying all of the Mastermind's shards in a single turn. In addition, the mastermind's wounded fury never once impacted me because every time I had enough power to fight him, I didn't have a discard pile at the time. It was a long game, and I only had 4 cards remaining in the villain deck, and luck carried me through. I feel like if I had enough time to try a second go, it wouldn't work out as well.

First attempt I didn't read the scheme win condition and invalidated my game by playing many turns past the ten shards on the mastermind. Also selected a hero that added shards to the game not realizing it put me on a faster clock. Also opened six henchman in a row and couldn't hit any of them cuz I never got  a 3 fight hand, kept drawing a wound and 2 fight, etc. Second game I encountered the same problem with not being able to hit a henchman but also opened double rare in the HQ after the opening mulligan so just had a dead HQ all game. Easily the worst least satisfying two games of legendary I've ever played lol
I thought I'd hate this setup, but thank goodness for The Ancient One!

Good way to start up again! First playthrough only had 3 turns left, was not very good at all. Second one went way better. Strikes didn't seem to come, although I was one lost duel away from losing the game. Luckily I was able to KO many troopers and agents. Clea and Brainstorm set up the Demonic Bargain front, accompanied by Dr. Strange. The other side focussed on Howard and FF heroes, making the man out of time go off almost every turn.
We learned a lot from our first game and we're able to recruit a more diverse set of heroes!

column33: Nice combination of heroes.  Combination of sets presented a nice challenge as well.

First game was a slog and only won on the second to last card. Second game started with 3 STs in the first 6 turns so was worried we were screwed, but we got lucky on colors for sonic frequency in the HQ and were able to make steady progress on Klaw. 

For hero selection we intentionally did not want any green cards since they didn’t help the scheme and took the risk of what it meant for Klaw. We were pretty happy with that strategy and chose Brainstorm and Shuri to manipulate our top card and HQ.

Because the three required heroes were so instinct/covert class dominated, and because this scheme required some tech/ranged to be avoid the mastermind winning duels, I went with heavy tech ranged heroes: Shuri and Brainstorm.  Brainstorm synced well with Psi-lord from a team standpoint, and Shuri's multi class cards plus the ability to change the HQ and ,therefore, empowering was valuable.  In hindsight, I regret not going more of an artifact route for protection from Duel losses (it only takes one in two player) as Strange's Cagliastro + Watatomb provides all the protection you need for the Duels as long as they aren't discarded.  However, was able to get an early Psi-lord rare, which when combined with Man out of TIme from Brainstorm/Psilord allowed it to continue staying in my hand, which then led to a Brainstorm rare, and then everything went downhill for evil from there.

Good setup, interesting pairing Lords of the Underworld with the Circus of Crime so there's some different top decking to think about.  I went with BP and White Wolf to get a lot of Hero Classes to mitigate the scheme.  Even with that choice, my first game had 6 Twists pop in 10 turns

MCU side settled on a single artifact and 4 Keeper of the Sanctums to keep the deck moving quickly and splashing Psi-Lords for symbols.  Comic side played Duck, Duck, Panther with Klaw and even with him pulling a BP card for his Final Blow, the Duck ran amuck and shut the party down before the fifth lost Duel.

Being away for Thanksgiving was a blessing and a curse. I had time to play which I think will be difficult with peak being...now...which was nice but I had to make quick choices for heroes. Thankfully Jin got their video up just before the time we left. I took their suggestions and ran. The first game was trash I couldn't control the HQ at all and only hit Zolo once. However, my second run I don't think Zolo got above 15 attack and stayed at 8 for the most part. I even got 1 6 hit on him. I was unsure about how well Captain Britain would be but with his 0+ it really helped.
